title: Comparing Academic Performance in Online and Face-to-Face Object-Oriented Programming Courses: An Educational Data Mining Analysis
journal: PROCEEDINGS OF 19TH IBERIAN CONFERENCE ON INFORMATION SYSTEMS AND TECHNOLOGIES, CISTI 2024, VOL 3
authors: Jorge Rodas-Silva et al.
summary_es: Este estudio compara el desempeño académico de estudiantes en cursos de programación orientada a objetos impartidos en modalidad virtual y presencial. Mediante técnicas de minería de datos educativos, identifica diferencias de rendimiento y patrones útiles para mejorar el diseño de la enseñanza de programación.
summary_en: This study compares student performance in online and face-to-face object-oriented programming courses. Using educational data mining techniques, it identifies achievement differences and learning patterns that can inform improvements in programming instruction.

title: Teaching programming in higher education: a bibliometric analysis of trends, technologies, and pedagogical approaches
journal: Frontiers in Education
authors: Mariuxi Vinueza-Morales, Jorge Rodas‐Silva, Cristian Vidal-Silva, Jorge Córdova-Morán, Edwin Cevallos-Ayón
abstract: The continuous evolution of digital technologies has significantly reshaped the landscape of higher education, particularly in programming instruction. As programming skills become increasingly essential across multiple disciplines, analyzing the research trends, key contributors, and institutional impact on programming education is crucial. This study presents a bibliometric analysis to identify influential research works, leading authors, and major institutions that have shaped this field over the years. By systematically examining scholarly publications, this study explores the most recurrent pedagogical approaches, the role of emerging technologies, and the methodological frameworks used to assess programming competencies. Additionally, it highlights prevailing research trends, knowledge gaps, and future directions for improving programming education in higher education institutions. The findings of this work will support educators, curriculum designers, and policymakers in refining instructional strategies, fostering innovative learning environments, and aligning academic programs with industry demands.
summary_es: The continuous evolution of digital technologies has significantly reshaped the landscape of higher education, particularly in programming instruction. As programming skills become increasingly essential across multiple disciplines, analyzing the research trends, key contributors, and institutional impact on programming education is crucial.
summary_en: The continuous evolution of digital technologies has significantly reshaped the landscape of higher education, particularly in programming instruction. As programming skills become increasingly essential across multiple disciplines, analyzing the research trends, key contributors, and institutional impact on programming education is crucial.

title: Teaching Programming in Higher Education: Analyzing Trends, Technologies, and Pedagogical Approaches Through a Bibliometric Lens
journal: International Journal of Advanced Computer Science and Applications
authors: Mariuxi Vinueza-Morales, Jorge Rodas‐Silva, Cristian Vidal-Silva
abstract: In today’s information society, developing programming competencies is essential in higher education. Numerous studies have been conducted on effective strategies for fostering these skills. This study performs a bibliometric analysis of research on teaching strategies for programming in higher education, using data from the SCOPUS and Web of Science (WOS) databases between 2014 and 2023. The analysis identifies key trends, influential authors, and collaboration networks in this field. The most effective teaching strategies include project-based learning, flipped classrooms, and collaborative programming. Emerging technologies such as augmented reality and virtual reality are gaining prominence in programming education. Despite the growth of research in this area, challenges remain, such as the lack of longitudinal studies exploring the long-term impact of these methodologies and the need for greater geographic diversity in studies. This paper emphasizes the importance of exploring new technologies and interdisciplinary approaches and fostering international collaborations to enhance programming education. The findings guide researchers and educators on how to optimize programming learning in a global context.
summary_es: In today’s information society, developing programming competencies is essential in higher education. Numerous studies have been conducted on effective strategies for fostering these skills.
summary_en: In today’s information society, developing programming competencies is essential in higher education. Numerous studies have been conducted on effective strategies for fostering these skills.

title: Computational Thinking and Academic Performance Across Different Instructional Modalities in Pre-University Courses: A Data-Driven Study
journal: Engineering Reports
authors: Jorge Parraga‐Alava, Jorge Rodas‐Silva
abstract: ABSTRACT In preuniversity education, educators and decision‐makers need to understand how teaching methods affect student learning in computational thinking (CT). This helps identify factors influencing student outcomes and inform the development of personalized learning programs. In this article, we conduct a comparative analysis of the academic performance of students enrolled in CT courses delivered through online (OL), blended (BL), and face‐to‐face (FTF) modalities in one public university in Ecuador. Our analysis focuses on preuniversity students during the academic year 2023. First, we collect data on student demographics and academic performance in each modality. Then, we applied statistical analysis to determine significant relationships between modalities. Next, we examine patterns and relationships between sociodemographic factors and academic results. Our results reveal that instructional modality has a significant impact on CT performance, with FTF students achieving better outcomes. Across all formats, admission scores and gender emerged as key predictors. While sociodemographic factors had greater influence in the FTF modality, academic factors played a more prominent role in BL and OL escenarios.
summary_es: In preuniversity education, educators and decision‐makers need to understand how teaching methods affect student learning in computational thinking (CT). This helps identify factors influencing student outcomes and inform the development of personalized learning programs.
summary_en: In preuniversity education, educators and decision‐makers need to understand how teaching methods affect student learning in computational thinking (CT). This helps identify factors influencing student outcomes and inform the development of personalized learning programs.

title: Bibliometric analysis of teaching strategies for programming courses in higher education,Análisis bibliométrico de estrategias de enseñanza para cursos de programación en educación superior
journal: Formacion Universitaria
authors: Mariuxi Vinueza-Morales, Jorge Rodas‐Silva, Cristian Vidal-Silva, Erika Madariaga-García
abstract: ResumenEste estudio analiza investigaciones sobre estrategias de enseanza en programacin, utilizando las bases de datos SCOPUS y Web of Science desde 2014 a 2023, para identificar tendencias clave, autores influyentes y redes de colaboracin.El desarrollo de competencias de programacin es esencial en la educacin superior.Los resultados muestran que las estrategias enseanza-aprendizaje ms implementadas son el aprendizaje basado en proyectos (37%), aula invertida (25%) y programacin colaborativa (18%).Tecnologas emergentes como la realidad aumentada y virtual estn cobrando importancia.A pesar de los avances tecnolgicos, metodolgicos e investigaciones en esta rea, an faltan estudios longitudinales para evaluar los impactos a largo plazo.Se destaca la necesidad de explorar tecnologas innovadoras, enfoques interdisciplinarios y el fomento de colaboraciones internacionales.En conclusin, este anlisis bibliomtrico proporciona una visin detallada de las tendencias actuales y los principales contribuyentes en la investigacin sobre la enseanza y el aprendizaje de la programacin en la educacin superior.

title: Enhancing Electric Power Consumption Prediction Using Machine Learning and Genetic Algorithms
journal: Etcm 2024 8th Ecuador Technical Chapters Meeting
authors: Jorge Parraga‐Alava, Jorge Rodas‐Silva
abstract: Electric power consumption prediction enables the development of smart strategies aimed at conserving energy, enhancing efficiency, and establishing sensible pricing mechanisms. In this paper, we used predictive models for electric power consumption using machine learning and genetic algorithm techniques. The dataset utilized consists of socio-demographic instances and power consumption-related measured values collected by Centrales Eléctricas de Nariño (CEDENAR), Colombia. Three predictive models—Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosting Machines (GBM), and Support Vector Machines (SVM)—were employed to predict power consumption. Additionally, a genetic algorithm (GA) was applied to optimize the parameter values of each model. Performance metrics, including mean absolute error (MAE), mean absolute percentage error (MAPE), root mean squared error (RMSE), and coefficient of determination ($\mathrm{R}^{2}$), were used to evaluate the effectiveness of the models. Our findings indicate that all models, both in their standalone forms and after genetic algorithm optimization, demonstrate improvements in performance metrics. However, statistically, the RF + GA model exhibits superiority only in the MAE and MAPE metrics, achieving an $\mathrm{R}^{2}$ of 0.76, reflecting its enhanced predictive capability in electric power consumption estimation. These results offer valuable insights for energy management strategies and resource planning in the context of electricity consumption.
summary_es: Electric power consumption prediction enables the development of smart strategies aimed at conserving energy, enhancing efficiency, and establishing sensible pricing mechanisms. In this paper, we used predictive models for electric power consumption using machine learning and genetic algorithm techniques.
summary_en: Electric power consumption prediction enables the development of smart strategies aimed at conserving energy, enhancing efficiency, and establishing sensible pricing mechanisms. In this paper, we used predictive models for electric power consumption using machine learning and genetic algorithm techniques.

title: Applying Educational Programming Language-Based Learning to Enhance the Programming Skills of Non-ICT Engineering College Students
journal: Human Behavior and Emerging Technologies
authors: Jorge Rodas‐Silva, Jorge Parraga‐Alava, Mariuxi Vinueza-Morales, Ana Eva Chacón-Luna, J. Cobo
abstract: The need for knowledgeable programmers has increased, highlighting the importance of strong programming foundations in engineering education. Limited access to high‐quality learning materials and educational opportunities presents challenges unrelated to information and communication technology (ICT) field (non‐ICT‐related) students in acquiring programming skills. Educational programming languages (EPLs) such as App Lab have gained popularity as they offer an accessible platform for students to learn programming fundamentals in a visual and interactive manner. This paper examines the impact of the EPL called App Lab on the development of fundamental programming skills among non‐ICT‐related field engineering college students. We conducted a quasiexperimental research study using a single‐blinded, nonequivalent control group pretest–posttest design. The study included 56 participants, all of whom were enrolled in the Environmental Engineering and Biotechnology program at the State University of Milagro (UNEMI), Ecuador. The experimental group consisted of 26 students, while the control group comprised 30 students. The assessment process involved the administration of a battery of 200 questions before and after the intervention. The intervention involved the use of App Lab as an EPL and lasted for a duration of 3 weeks exclusively for the experimental group, while the control group followed their usual tutoring program. The study results showed that students who received EPL‐mediated learning with App Lab had significant increase in their programming skills. App Lab demonstrated a positive impact, particularly among male students who reported Internet usage, as well as in advanced programming topics including loops, lists, and functions, when compared to their female counterparts.
summary_es: The need for knowledgeable programmers has increased, highlighting the importance of strong programming foundations in engineering education. Limited access to high‐quality learning materials and educational opportunities presents challenges unrelated to information and communication technology (ICT) field (non‐ICT‐related) students in acquiring programming skills.
summary_en: The need for knowledgeable programmers has increased, highlighting the importance of strong programming foundations in engineering education. Limited access to high‐quality learning materials and educational opportunities presents challenges unrelated to information and communication technology (ICT) field (non‐ICT‐related) students in acquiring programming skills.

title: An effective approach for identifying keywords as high-quality filters to get emergency-implicated Twitter Spanish data
journal: COMPUTER SPEECH AND LANGUAGE
authors: Joel Garcia-Arteaga, Jesús Zambrano-Zambrano, Jorge Parraga‐Alava, Jorge Rodas‐Silva
summary_es: El artículo propone un método para identificar palabras clave capaces de filtrar mensajes en español de Twitter relacionados con emergencias. La estrategia busca mejorar la calidad de los datos recopilados para apoyar sistemas de monitoreo, clasificación y respuesta ante incidentes.
summary_en: The article proposes a method for identifying keywords that effectively filter Spanish-language Twitter posts related to emergencies. The approach improves the quality of collected data for incident monitoring, classification, and emergency-response applications.

title: A Tuning Approach Using Genetic Algorithms for Emergency Incidents Classification in Social Media in Ecuador
authors: Joel Garcia-Arteaga, Jesús Zambrano-Zambrano, Jorge Parraga‐Alava, Jorge Rodas‐Silva
summary_es: Este capítulo presenta un enfoque de ajuste mediante algoritmos genéticos para clasificar incidentes de emergencia publicados en redes sociales en Ecuador. La optimización de parámetros busca aumentar la precisión de los modelos y facilitar la detección oportuna de información relevante para la gestión de emergencias.
summary_en: This chapter presents a genetic-algorithm tuning approach for classifying emergency incidents reported on social media in Ecuador. Parameter optimization is used to improve model accuracy and support timely detection of information relevant to emergency management.

title: Opinion and Sentiment Analysis of Twitter Users during the 2021 Ecuador Presidential Election
journal: PROCEEDINGS OF THE 17TH INTERNATIONAL CONFERENCE ON WEB INFORMATION SYSTEMS AND TECHNOLOGIES (WEBIST)
authors: Jorge Parraga‐Alava, Jorge Rodas‐Silva, Iván Quimi, Roberth Alcívar-Cevallos
summary_es: La investigación analiza opiniones y sentimientos expresados por usuarios de Twitter durante las elecciones presidenciales de Ecuador de 2021. El estudio aplica técnicas de procesamiento de lenguaje natural para reconocer tendencias, polaridad y patrones de conversación política en redes sociales.
summary_en: This research analyzes opinions and sentiments expressed by Twitter users during Ecuador's 2021 presidential election. Natural language processing techniques are applied to identify trends, polarity, and patterns in political conversations on social media.

title: El uso de aplicaciones Web para la Gestión de clínicas veterinarias y su incidencia en la mejora de procesos administrativos
journal: Ecuadorian Science Journal
authors: Anthony Cedeno Ochoa, Axelman Catuto Murillo, Jorge Rodas‐Silva
abstract: Actualmente en Ecuador, el problema que existe en el ámbito de la medicina veterinaria, es que en la mayoría de las clínicas no cuentan con herramientas tecnológicas para llevar una gestión centralizada y digitalizada de la información, siendo que aún lo realizan de forma manual lo que conlleva a que la organización de los expedientes almacenados se dificulte y en muchos casos ocasione la pérdida o la duplicidad de información. Este trabajo tiene como objetivo mostrar una aplicación web mediante el uso de software libre para la administración de información en clínicas veterinarias que contribuya al mejoramiento de los procesos administrativos y facilite el trabajo a los empleados. En cuanto a la metodología utilizada para el desarrollo de esta aplicación, fue un modelo de proceso iterativo e incremental, el cual permitió lograr los objetivos propuestos y entregar un producto de calidad que resuelva la problemática existente en las veterinarias. Por lo tanto, el resultado de esta aplicación web generó los siguientes beneficios: reducir el tiempo de atención a las mascotas, acceder a la información de manera ágil, lograr que el manejo de la información sea más eficiente, mejorar la búsqueda de registros, así como la gestión de expedientes clínicos y aportar con el cuidado del medio ambiente al reducir la generación de desperdicios. Finalmente, se propone esta aplicación web como una solución para mejorar los procesos manejados en la clínica veterinaria, todo con el fin de incorporar nuevas tecnologías en esta área para alcanzar su máximo desarrollo y rentabilidad.
summary_es: Actualmente en Ecuador, el problema que existe en el ámbito de la medicina veterinaria, es que en la mayoría de las clínicas no cuentan con herramientas tecnológicas para llevar una gestión centralizada y digitalizada de la información, siendo que aún lo realizan de forma manual lo que conlleva a que la organización de los expedientes almacenados se dificulte y en muchos casos ocasione la pérdida o la duplicidad de información. Este trabajo tiene como objetivo mostrar una aplicación web mediante el uso de…

Este trabajo tiene como objetivo mostrar una aplicación web mediante el uso de…

title: Teaching programming with MIT app inventor: A literature review,Enseñanza de programación mediante MIT App Inventor: Una revisión de literatura
journal: Proceedings of the LACCEI international Multi-conference for Engineering, Education and Technology
authors: Mariuxi Vinueza-Morales, Ana Eva Chacón-Luna, Jorge Rodas‐Silva, Héctor Serrano Mantilla
abstract: MIT App Inventor is a visual block programming environment created for mobile application development by novice programmers, and is widely used around the world for learning programming. This paper describes the literature review conducted by applying search and selection criteria, using specialized search engines, followed by the analysis of the 35 selected studies (experiments and experiences) on the teaching-learning process of programming conducted by teachers from various countries, using App Inventor. The findings indicate a high acceptance in the academic community of App Inventor as an effective tool for motivation and performance of students who are initiated in programming, without distinction in the educational level.
summary_es: MIT App Inventor is a visual block programming environment created for mobile application development by novice programmers, and is widely used around the world for learning programming. This paper describes the literature review conducted by applying search and selection criteria, using specialized search engines, followed by the analysis of the 35 selected studies (experiments and experiences) on the teaching-learning process of programming conducted by teachers from various countries, using App Inventor.
summary_en: MIT App Inventor is a visual block programming environment created for mobile application development by novice programmers, and is widely used around the world for learning programming. This paper describes the literature review conducted by applying search and selection criteria, using specialized search engines, followed by the analysis of the 35 selected studies (experiments and experiences) on the teaching-learning process of programming conducted by teachers from various countries, using App Inventor.

title: Using Alice software as a tool for programming learning: A literature review,El uso del software Alice como herramienta para el aprendizaje de programación: Una revisión de literatura
journal: Proceedings of the LACCEI international Multi-conference for Engineering, Education and Technology
authors: Jorge Antonio Córdova Morán, Jorge Rodas‐Silva, Mariuxi G. Vinueza Morales
abstract: Alice is one of the most used tools in learning programming languages, given its usefulness to acquire skills in problem solving and algorithmic thinking. This document presents the results of the analysis of the use of the Alice platform as a tool for learning programming from a literature review. We used the most common databases in the area of computer science such as ACM, IEE, among others; and taking into account a search protocol, and some criteria, 24 articles were selected. Among the findings are the effectiveness of the use of Alice as a learning resource for introduction to programming, the amount of work in English, and the weak validity of the experimental results.
summary_es: Alice is one of the most used tools in learning programming languages, given its usefulness to acquire skills in problem solving and algorithmic thinking. This document presents the results of the analysis of the use of the Alice platform as a tool for learning programming from a literature review.
summary_en: Alice is one of the most used tools in learning programming languages, given its usefulness to acquire skills in problem solving and algorithmic thinking. This document presents the results of the analysis of the use of the Alice platform as a tool for learning programming from a literature review.

title: Selection of Software Product Line Implementation Components Using Recommender Systems: An Application to Wordpress
journal: IEEE ACCESS
authors: Jorge Rodas‐Silva, José Á. Galindo, Jorge Garcia-Gutierrez, David Benavides
abstract: In software products line (SPL), there may be features which can be implemented by different components, which means there are several implementations for the same feature. In this context, the selection of the best components set to implement a given configuration is a challenging task due to the high number of combinations and options which could be selected. In certain scenarios, it is possible to find information associated with the components which could help in this selection task, such as user ratings. In this paper, we introduce a component-based recommender system, called (REcommender System that suggests implementation Components from selecteD fEatures), which uses information associated with the implementation components to make recommendations in the domain of the SPL configuration. We also provide a RESDEC reference implementation that supports collaborative-based and content-based filtering algorithms to recommend (i.e., implementation components) regarding WordPress-based websites configuration. The empirical results, on a knowledge base with 680 plugins and 187 000 ratings by 116 000 users, show promising results. Concretely, this indicates that it is possible to guide the user throughout the implementation components selection with a margin of error smaller than 13% according to our evaluation.
summary_es: In software products line (SPL), there may be features which can be implemented by different components, which means there are several implementations for the same feature. In this context, the selection of the best components set to implement a given configuration is a challenging task due to the high number of combinations and options which could be selected.
summary_en: In software products line (SPL), there may be features which can be implemented by different components, which means there are several implementations for the same feature. In this context, the selection of the best components set to implement a given configuration is a challenging task due to the high number of combinations and options which could be selected.

title: RESDEC: Online Management Tool for Implementation Components Selection in Software Product Lines Using Recommender Systems
journal: 23RD INTERNATIONAL SYSTEMS AND SOFTWARE PRODUCT LINE CONFERENCE(SPLC 2019), VOL B
authors: Jorge Rodas‐Silva, José Á. Galindo, Jorge García–Gutiérrez, David Benavides
abstract: Software product lines (SPL) management is one of the most important activities for the software engineer and it represents one of the key pieces of software product line engineering. When a software system grow fast, configuring a product becomes a costly and error-prone activity due to the amount of features available for configuration. This process becomes more complex when for each feature, there is more than one component that implements it. Currently the tools available for configuration management do not have automated mechanisms to facilitate the optimal components selection that meet the functions required by a given product. In this paper, we introduce a prototype component-based recommender system called RESDEC (REcommender System that suggest implementation Components from selecteD fEatures) designed to manage the best implementation components alternatives. Our tool is validated using WordPress-based websites where the implementation components are represented by plugins and the recommendations generated by RESDEC help interested parties in the search and efficient plugins selection to configure websites.
summary_es: Software product lines (SPL) management is one of the most important activities for the software engineer and it represents one of the key pieces of software product line engineering. When a software system grow fast, configuring a product becomes a costly and error-prone activity due to the amount of features available for configuration.
summary_en: Software product lines (SPL) management is one of the most important activities for the software engineer and it represents one of the key pieces of software product line engineering. When a software system grow fast, configuring a product becomes a costly and error-prone activity due to the amount of features available for configuration.

title: Automated analysis of two-layered feature models with feature attributes
journal: Journal of Computer Languages
authors: Michael Lettner, Jorge Rodas‐Silva, José Á. Galindo, David Benavides
summary_es: El trabajo propone un procedimiento automatizado para analizar modelos de características de dos niveles que incluyen atributos. Su contribución facilita la validación de configuraciones y el razonamiento sobre variabilidad en líneas de productos de software complejas.
summary_en: The work proposes an automated procedure for analyzing two-layer feature models that include feature attributes. It supports configuration validation and reasoning about variability in complex software product lines.

title: Selection of deployment configurations using Recommender Systems on Android,Selección de configuraciones de despliegue usando Sistemas de Recomendación en Android
journal: RISTI - Revista Iberica de Sistemas e Tecnologias de Informacao
authors: Jorge Rodas-Silva et al.
summary_es: El artículo estudia el uso de sistemas de recomendación para seleccionar configuraciones de despliegue en aplicaciones Android. La propuesta ayuda a orientar decisiones entre múltiples alternativas considerando las características y preferencias asociadas al contexto de implementación.
summary_en: This article studies the use of recommender systems to select deployment configurations for Android applications. The approach supports decisions among multiple alternatives by considering characteristics and preferences associated with the implementation context.

title: RESDEC: Un prototipo de herramienta para la selección de configuraciones de despliegue basado en Sistemas de Recomendación
journal: Actas de las 23rd Jornadas de Ingenieria del Software y Bases de Datos, JISBD 2018
authors: Jorge Rodas-Silva et al.
summary_es: El trabajo presenta RESDEC, un prototipo que utiliza sistemas de recomendación para apoyar la selección de configuraciones de despliegue. La herramienta busca reducir la complejidad de elección y ofrecer alternativas acordes con las necesidades y preferencias del usuario.
summary_en: The paper presents RESDEC, a prototype that uses recommender systems to support deployment-configuration selection. The tool reduces choice complexity and recommends alternatives aligned with user needs and preferences.

title: Programming with the SCRATCH + CARAMBA tool. A significant learning experience,Programación con la herramienta SCRATCH +CARAMBA. Una experiencia de aprendizaje significativo
journal: Proceedings of the LACCEI international Multi-conference for Engineering, Education and Technology
authors: Jorge Rodas-Silva et al.
summary_es: El estudio describe una experiencia de aprendizaje significativo de programación mediante las herramientas Scratch y CARAMBA. La propuesta emplea entornos visuales e interactivos para favorecer la comprensión de conceptos computacionales y aumentar la participación estudiantil.
summary_en: The study describes a meaningful programming-learning experience using the Scratch and CARAMBA tools. Visual and interactive environments are used to strengthen understanding of computational concepts and increase student engagement.

title: Hacia el uso de sistemas de recomendación en sistemas de alta variabilidad
journal: Actas de las 21st Jornadas de Ingenier&amp;amp;amp;amp;amp;#65533;a del Software y Bases de Datos, JISBD 2016
authors: Jorge Rodas‐Silva, Javier Olivares, José Ángel Galindo Duarte, David Benavides
abstract: Los sistemas de alta variabilidad son sistemas software cuyo comportamiento puede ser personalizado de acuerdo con las necesidades de un contexto particular. De hecho, existen sistemas de alta variabilidad que representan miles de productos. Por otra parte, en la industria encontramos los sistemas de recomendación, los cuales, permiten recomendar la serie de productos que mejor se adapten a un usuario o un contexto de uso particular. En este artículo de prospección exploramos el uso de sistemas de recomendación en el contexto de los sistemas de alta variabilidad. Asimismo, identificamos algunas tareas donde podrían ayudar a la gestión de los sistemas de variabilidad.
summary_es: Los sistemas de alta variabilidad son sistemas software cuyo comportamiento puede ser personalizado de acuerdo con las necesidades de un contexto particular. De hecho, existen sistemas de alta variabilidad que representan miles de productos.
summary_en: Los sistemas de alta variabilidad son sistemas software cuyo comportamiento puede ser personalizado de acuerdo con las necesidades de un contexto particular. De hecho, existen sistemas de alta variabilidad que representan miles de productos.

title: El uso de modelos de características con atributos para pruebas en sistemas de alta variabilidad: Primeros pasos
journal: Actas de las 21st Jornadas de Ingenier&amp;amp;amp;amp;amp;#65533;a del Software y Bases de Datos, JISBD 2016
authors: Mariuxi Vinueza, Jorge Rodas‐Silva, José Ángel Galindo Duarte, David Benavides
summary_es: International audience
summary_en: International audience

title: Body Bias usage in UTBB FDSOI designs: A parametric exploration approach
journal: SOLID-STATE ELECTRONICS
authors: Jorge Rodas-Silva et al.
summary_es: La investigación explora paramétricamente el uso de polarización del cuerpo en diseños UTBB FDSOI. Evalúa cómo esta técnica puede ajustar el comportamiento eléctrico de los dispositivos y contribuir al equilibrio entre rendimiento, consumo energético y variaciones de fabricación.
summary_en: The research parametrically explores body-bias use in UTBB FDSOI designs. It evaluates how the technique adjusts device electrical behavior and contributes to balancing performance, energy consumption, and manufacturing variations.

title: Towards testing variability intensive systems using user reviews,Hacia las pruebas en sistemas de alta variabilidad utilizando opiniones de los usuarios
journal: 2015 10th Colombian Computing Conference, 10CCC 2015
authors: Jorge Rodas‐Silva, David Méndez-Acuña, José Á. Galindo, David Benavides, Jesennia Cardenas
abstract: Variability intensive systems are software systems that describe a large set of diverse and different configurations that share some characteristics. This high number of configurations makes testing such systems an expensive and error-prone task. For example, in the Android ecosystem we can find up to 24 different valid configurations, thus, making it impossible to test an application on all of them. To alleviate this problem, previous research suggest the selection of a subset of test cases that maximize the changes of finding errors while maximizing the diversity of configurations. Concretely, the proposals focus on the prioritization and selection of tests, so only relevant configurations are tested according to some criterion. In this paper, we envision the use of user reports to prioritize and select meaningful tests. To do this, we explore the use of recommender systems as a possible improvement to the selection of test cases in intensive variability systems.
summary_es: Variability intensive systems are software systems that describe a large set of diverse and different configurations that share some characteristics. This high number of configurations makes testing such systems an expensive and error-prone task.
summary_en: Variability intensive systems are software systems that describe a large set of diverse and different configurations that share some characteristics. This high number of configurations makes testing such systems an expensive and error-prone task.

title: Towards testing variability intensive systems using user reviews
journal: 2015 10TH COMPUTING COLOMBIAN CONFERENCE (10CCC)
authors: Jorge Rodas‐Silva, David Méndez-Acuña, José Á. Galindo, David Benavides, Jesennia Cardenas
abstract: Variability intensive systems are software systems that describe a large set of diverse and different configurations that share some characteristics. This high number of configurations makes testing such systems an expensive and error-prone task. For example, in the Android ecosystem we can find up to 24 different valid configurations, thus, making it impossible to test an application on all of them. To alleviate this problem, previous research suggest the selection of a subset of test cases that maximize the changes of finding errors while maximizing the diversity of configurations. Concretely, the proposals focus on the prioritization and selection of tests, so only relevant configurations are tested according to some criterion. In this paper, we envision the use of user reports to prioritize and select meaningful tests. To do this, we explore the use of recommender systems as a possible improvement to the selection of test cases in intensive variability systems.
summary_es: Variability intensive systems are software systems that describe a large set of diverse and different configurations that share some characteristics. This high number of configurations makes testing such systems an expensive and error-prone task.
summary_en: Variability intensive systems are software systems that describe a large set of diverse and different configurations that share some characteristics. This high number of configurations makes testing such systems an expensive and error-prone task.

title: Evaluation and Mitigation of Aging Effects on a Digital On-Chip Voltage and Temperature Sensor
journal: PROCEEDINGS 2015 25TH INTERNATIONAL WORKSHOP ON POWER AND TIMING MODELING, OPTIMIZATION AND SIMULATION
authors: Jorge Rodas-Silva et al.
summary_es: El trabajo evalúa los efectos del envejecimiento sobre un sensor digital integrado de voltaje y temperatura. Además, analiza medidas de mitigación orientadas a preservar la precisión y confiabilidad del sensor durante su vida útil.
summary_en: The work evaluates aging effects on a digital on-chip voltage and temperature sensor. It also examines mitigation measures intended to preserve sensor accuracy and reliability throughout its operating lifetime.
